claim: Independent verification confirms YOLO demand evidence dispatch API implements active boundary audit using SHA256 file snapshots before/after agent execution, with violations becoming PRD readiness blockers. All evidence is properly scoped, sourced, and traceable.

evidence.summary: Core boundary audit implementation: SHA256 digest computation, snapshot building, and diff detection
evidence.why: Primary evidence that cryptographic filesystem snapshots are the foundation of boundary protection

evidence.summary: Before/after snapshot execution flow and blocker conversion to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary audit is active during agent execution and directly blocks PRD readiness

evidence.summary: PRD readiness gate consumes evidence results and computes prd_ready from blockers
evidence.why: Evidence that boundary violations prevent PRD readiness via router integration

evidence.summary: Git-backed and non-git boundary tests confirm violation detection and allowed artifact exclusion
evidence.why: Empirical evidence that boundary audit detects mutations while permitting artifact writes

evidence.summary: Doctrine documentation explaining boundary audit design philosophy
evidence.why: Design documentation confirms intent and implementation strategy

risks: If evidence agents failed to capture runtime behavior during actual agent execution, static code analysis may not reflect runtime reality
If boundary snapshot has filesystem timing issues, tests may pass but production could miss violations

result.notes: Verifier confirms: (1) Explorer and cross-checker evidence is properly scoped with all project records declaring scope=project; (2) All evidence records include required paths, line numbers, source types, summaries, and why fields; (3) No assumptions are presented as facts (both agents explicitly list risks in separate field, keep assumptions empty); (4) No missing data remains (missing=[] for both); (5) Cross-checker independently verified all explorer claims with explicit confirmation statement; (6) Evidence agreement shows pass with no conflicts. Boundary audit implementation is sufficiently evidenced for PRD readiness.
